_ai: AI fills this. Derived from: personas.json + functional-requirements.json + non-functional-requirements.json + business-rules.json. Do not edit manually.

authentication.mechanism: JWT (JSON Web Tokens) — RSA-256 signed, validated at api-gateway

optional: correlation_id (set at gateway, propagated to all services)

expiry.rationale: Short-lived access tokens limit blast radius of token theft

publicEndpoints: POST /tenants/{tenantId}/bookings (customer booking — email/phone as identity, no account required)

enterpriseSso.notes: Enterprise tenants can federate with their identity provider. JWT claims are normalised at the gateway.

authorisation.enforcementLayer: api-gateway validates role claim; individual services re-validate for sensitive operations

roles.scope: Cross-tenant — can act on any tenant
Own tenant only — tenant_id from JWT is the boundary
permissions: View and manage all bookings for their tenant
Cannot access other tenant data — enforced by RLS

roles.scope: Own tenant only — limited to their own staff_id for write operations
Own bookings only — identified by customer_id derived from email
permissions: Create, cancel, and reschedule own bookings

tenantIsolation.description: All tenants share a single PostgreSQL cluster. RLS policies on every table ensure queries only return rows belonging to the current tenant.

enforcementLayers.mechanism: SET LOCAL app.tenant_id = $1 at the start of every transaction. RLS policies filter by current_setting('app.tenant_id'). NULL context returns zero rows by default — safe-by-default posture (BR005).
api-gateway extracts tenant_id from validated JWT and injects it into the request context. All service handlers propagate tenant_id from context — never from request body.
All Redis keys prefixed with tenant_id: (e.g. tenant_id:{uuid}:availability:{staff_id}:{date}). Cross-tenant cache reads are structurally impossible.
enforcementLayers.coverage: ElastiCache slot availability cache and session store

enforcementLayers.mechanism: All events must carry tenant_id in the envelope (BR013). EventEnvelope validator in all consumers rejects events with missing or mismatched tenant_id and routes to DLQ.
Automated SQL probe runs every 5 minutes in production, executing cross-tenant queries that should return zero rows. Any non-zero result fires a P1 alert (NFR012).

inTransit.protocol: TLS 1.3 minimum on all ingress (ALB to clients)
inTransit.internalServices: VPC private subnet communication; TLS between ECS tasks for sensitive service-to-service calls

atRest.database: RDS AES-256 encryption (AWS KMS managed key)

piiHandling.scope: Customer PII (name, email, phone) stored per-tenant, never globally
piiHandling.erasure: GDPR right-to-erasure: customer PII pseudonymised in place (name → REDACTED, email → erasure-{uuid}@bookvault.invalid, phone → null). Appointment event log and audit log retain structural records with PII replaced.
piiHandling.exportSecurity: Tenant data exports delivered via signed S3 URL (24-hour expiry) sent to verified owner email only (FR012)

secrets.name: bookvault/db/credentials
secrets.rotation: Automatic every 30 days via Lambda rotation function

secrets.name: bookvault/jwt/signing-key
secrets.rotation: Manual, blue-green rotation — new key published, 15-minute overlap to drain in-flight tokens

secrets.name: bookvault/stripe/api-key
secrets.rotation: Manual per Stripe best practice; zero-downtime via Secrets Manager versioning

secretsManagement.accessControl: IAM roles per ECS task — each service's task role grants access only to the secrets it needs. No wildcard Secret policies.
secretsManagement.auditTrail: All Secrets Manager access logged to CloudTrail; alerts on anomalous access patterns
